rationale: The plan is well-scoped and additive. It creates one new pure module (evolution.ts) and makes minimal, non-breaking changes to two existing files (renderLoop.ts, types.ts). The approach leverages existing infrastructure (createPRNG, VisualParams, FrameState) without modifying their contracts. The sinusoidal modulation strategy is stateless per-frame (aside from memoized curve params), which aligns with the project's in-memory-only, no-persistence philosophy.

create.notes: Pure function module with no side effects. Depends only on existing createPRNG and VisualParams. Memoization by seed string is appropriate since seed is stable per session.
Test file only. Comprehensive coverage plan with 9 test cases covering determinism, smoothness, range bounds, seed variance, and reduced-motion preservation.

modify.notes: Changes are minimal: track startTime, compute elapsed, call evolveParams after mapSignalsToVisuals, pass elapsed in FrameState. The existing FrameState already has a 'time' field (elapsed ms) and 'delta' field, so the elapsed tracking is already partially in place. The plan correctly skips evolution in the fallback branch.

Plan says to add 'elapsed' field to FrameState, but FrameState already has 'time: number' described as 'Elapsed time in ms'. This is a redundant change — the existing 'time' field already serves this purpose. The plan should use the existing 'time' field rather than adding a new 'elapsed' field.

concerns.description: FrameState already contains 'time: number' (elapsed time in ms). Adding a separate 'elapsed' field is redundant. The plan should use the existing 'time' field instead.
concerns.recommendation: Skip the types.ts modification in step 2. Use FrameState.time as the elapsed value when calling evolveParams in renderLoop.ts.

concerns.description: The plan imports createPRNG from './prng' but references it as being from the mappings module in some places. The actual location is src/visual/prng.ts.
concerns.recommendation: Import from './prng' as stated in step 1 details — this is correct.

concerns.description: The plan correctly preserves motionAmplitude from reduced-motion but should also ensure pointerDisturbance, bassEnergy, and trebleEnergy are not modulated by evolution since these are real-time reactive signals, not seed-derived base parameters.
concerns.recommendation: evolveParams should only modulate seed-derived params (paletteHue, paletteSaturation, cadence) and explicitly pass through all real-time reactive fields unchanged.

dependencyCheck.US-007: Assumed satisfied — renderLoop.ts and mappings.ts are fully implemented with audio integration
dependencyCheck.US-009: Assumed satisfied — particleField geometry system is complete with bass/treble distinction

testCoverage.notes: 9 new evolution tests plus ~3 updated renderLoop tests. Coverage plan is thorough and tests all acceptance criteria.

Elapsed time changes at least two visual.how: paletteHue and cadence both modulated by elapsed time; paletteSaturation as optional third

Scene does not hard reset during runtime.how: Continuous sinusoidal curves with no discontinuities; smoothness test enforces bounded frame-to-frame deltas

Temporal behavior differs between sessio.how: createPRNG seeded with session seed generates unique amplitudes, frequencies, and phase offsets per session

Motion remains smooth.how: Layered sine waves with bounded amplitudes; explicit smoothness test scanning consecutive millisecond samples

performanceImpact: negligible — memoized curve params mean per-frame cost is only a few sine evaluations (6-9 calls for 2-3 params × 2-3 harmonics each)
securityImpact: none — no new external data sources, no storage APIs, no network calls
